text: Entering this space, I feel a sense of anticipation mixed with curiosity. The stark glass doors form a threshold between the ordinary corridor and a world of ideas and experiment. On the other side, the vivid wall demands attention—"R+D" blazes out, inviting thought and exploration. As I read the screen, the words resonate: exposed, confused, misread intentions. I feel a kinship with the described inner awkwardness, sensing my own vulnerability as I cross from passerby into participant. Here, the boundary between observer and observed blurs, and I am reminded how meaning is always being negotiated—not just through what I do, but also how I am seen. It is humbling, and a little thrilling.
